title: Matrix Arithmetic: Addition, Subtraction, and Multiplication
summary: Perform matrix addition, scalar multiplication, and matrix multiplication; understand when these operations are defined.
content_html:
Matrix operations follow specific rules about size compatibility.

Addition and Subtraction

Only matrices of the same size can be added or subtracted — element by element:

[1 2] + [5 6] = [1+5  2+6] = [6  8]
[3 4]   [7 8]   [3+7  4+8]   [10 12]

Scalar Multiplication

Multiply every element by the scalar k:

3 × [1 2] = [3  6]
    [3 4]   [9 12]

Matrix Multiplication

Matrix A (m×n) × Matrix B (n×p) = Matrix C (m×p). The number of columns in A must equal rows in B:

C[i][j] = sum of (A[i][k] × B[k][j]) for k = 1..n

Example: (2×2) × (2×2)

[1 2] × [5 6] = [1×5+2×7  1×6+2×8] = [19 22]
[3 4]   [7 8]   [3×5+4×7  3×6+4×8]   [43 50]

Important Properties

  • Matrix multiplication is NOT commutative: AB ≠ BA (in general)
  • Matrix multiplication IS associative: A(BC) = (AB)C
  • Identity: AI = IA = A
  • Distributive: A(B+C) = AB + AC

exercises.title: Addition condition
exercises.prompt: Two matrices can be added only if they have:
options: The same size (same rows and columns)
The same number of rows only
exercises.answer: The same size (same rows and columns)

exercises.title: Element calculation
exercises.prompt: In matrix multiplication C = AB, element C[i][j] equals:
options: The dot product of row i of A and column j of B
The sum of column i of A and row j of B
exercises.answer: The dot product of row i of A and column j of B

exercises.title: Distributive
exercises.prompt: Which property holds for matrix multiplication?
